hawkish_phrases: risks to the growth outlook remained tilted to the downside, but having become somewhat less pronounced
members reiterated the need for a highly accommodative monetary policy stance for a prolonged period of time
some members highlighted the need to be attentive to the possible side effects of the present monetary policy measures
some concern was also voiced with regard to the potential impact of negative interest rates on euro area households
needed to be emphasised that the analysis and monitoring of side effects was part and parcel of the Governing Council's ongoing monetary policy assessment
dovish_phrases: inflation developments had remained subdued overall
market-based measures of inflation expectations had stagnated at historically low levels
downside risks to the growth outlook continued to prevail
Projected inflation was generally seen to remain some distance from the Governing Council's inflation aim
the Governing Council stood ready to adjust all of its instruments, as appropriate, to ensure that inflation moved towards the aim in a sustained manner
summary: The Governing Council unanimously held all policy rates unchanged (deposit rate at -0.50%) with forward guidance indicating rates would remain at present or lower levels until inflation robustly converges near 2%, emphasizing patience while monitoring for side effects but expressing readiness to adjust all instruments if needed.
